mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns show positive direction in >65% of periods when FEDFUNDS increases, or Rank IC < 0.02 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An unemployment rate crossing above 4.0% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending capacity, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >60% of instances when UNRATE exceeds 4.0%, or the signal loses predictive power (Rank IC < 0.03) during periods of stable inflation.

mechanism_narrative: Strong retail sales growth combined with stable or declining federal funds rates indicates robust consumer demand without monetary suppression, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when RSAFS growth exceeds 5% annually while FEDFUNDS remains above 5.0%, or interaction term Rank IC < 0.01 over 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An index of industrial production expanding above its 12-month moving average reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO expansion fails to predict positive 5-day returns when global supply chain disruptions are active, or the signal directional accuracy drops below 50% in rolling 1-year tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product directly increases corporate revenue expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth rates show no statistical correlation with 60-day forward returns (Rank IC < 0.02) or produce negative returns in >55% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPI increase above 0.4% forces markets to price in aggressive central bank tightening, which compresses equity multiples and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: High inflation prints fail to trigger negative returns when accompanied by simultaneous rate cuts, or the threshold signal yields a directional accuracy below 45% over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous growth in total nonfarm payrolls and housing starts indicates synchronized labor and capital investment expansion, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal loses predictive power when HOUST growth is driven by speculative bubbles rather than fundamentals, or interaction Rank IC falls below 0.02 across 2-year out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: A contraction in industrial production during a late-cycle expansion signals capacity constraints and impending economic slowdown, which triggers negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO contractions fail to predict negative 5-day returns when accompanied by strong export demand, or the signal directional accuracy drops below 48% in rolling 1-year evaluations.
